name: Tristan
description: Tristan, also known as Tristram, is a legendary hero and one of the Knights of the Round Table in Arthurian legend. He is best known for his tragic love affair with Iseult (Isolde, Yseult). Born in Lyonesse (often associated with Cornwall), Tristan's parents died when he was young, and he was raised by his loyal tutor, Gorvenal. He proved to be a skilled warrior, hunter, and musician. Tristan is sent to Ireland to bring back Iseult to marry his uncle, King Mark of Cornwall. However, Tristan and Iseult accidentally drink a love potion and fall deeply in love. Their passionate but forbidden love leads to numerous trials, betrayals, and ultimately, their deaths. Tristan's story explores themes of courtly love, loyalty, betrayal, and the destructive power of uncontrollable passion. His tale has been retold in countless poems, novels, operas, and films, solidifying his place as a quintessential figure in romantic literature and a tragic hero of the Arthurian Cycle. His skill with a sword and his deep connection to music and poetry are other important parts of his legend.
subtitle: Knight of the Round Table, tragic lover
achievements: Becoming a Knight of the Round Table

keyMyths.title: The Love Potion
keyMyths.description: Tristan and Iseult accidentally drink a love potion intended for Iseult and King Mark, leading to their passionate and forbidden love. This event sets the stage for the tragedy that unfolds.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of Tristan and Iseult

keyMyths.title: Tristan's Death
keyMyths.description: Tristan is mortally wounded, often by a poisoned weapon. He sends for Iseult, whose white sail will signal her arrival, but his jealous wife lies and says the sail is black, leading to Tristan's death from despair.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of Tristan and Iseult

keyMyths.title: Slaying the Morholt
keyMyths.description: Tristan defeats the Morholt, a fearsome Irish knight, in single combat. This victory establishes Tristan's reputation as a skilled warrior and hero of Cornwall.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of Tristan and Iseult

sources.author: Thomas of Britain
sources.description: One of the earliest and most influential versions of the Tristan and Iseult story.
sources.text: One of the earliest and most influential versions of the Tristan and Iseult story.

sources.author: Gottfried von Strassburg
sources.description: An unfinished but highly regarded Middle High German version of the tale.
sources.text: An unfinished but highly regarded Middle High German version of the tale.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: Includes Tristan as a Knight of the Round Table and incorporates elements of his love affair with Iseult.
sources.text: Includes Tristan as a Knight of the Round Table and incorporates elements of his love affair with Iseult.
